title: First Reviewer Feedback Window / Feedback Capture
context: GrantLayer is publicly readable on GitHub in a developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture following GL-176 through GL-184. GL-184 produced the first external reviewer invite pack. GL-185 opens the controlled feedback window and defines the operational plan for manually inviting 2-5 reviewers and capturing responses. No outreach was sent by this issue.

feedback_window.purpose: Collect controlled qualitative feedback from 2-5 carefully selected external reviewers on GrantLayer's developer preview. Identify blockers, confusing documentation, broken quickstart paths, or missing examples before widening the reviewer pool.
feedback_window.suggested_length: 5-10 business days from the date the first invite is sent
feedback_window.feedback_capture: manual/internal recording only — no automated collection, no public form, no webhook or API collection
feedback_window.window_open_signal: When the user sends the first invite manually
feedback_window.window_close_signal: When the window length expires or 2-5 responses have been captured, whichever comes first

reviewer_invitation_procedure.method: manual only — no automated outreach performed by this issue
reviewer_invitation_procedure.invite_pack_reference: GL-184 first external reviewer invite pack (docs/first_external_reviewer_invite_pack.md, section 2)
reviewer_invitation_procedure.safety_reminder: Verify before sending: no secrets requested, no customer data requested, no production SaaS claim, no tenant isolation claim, no exploit details requested, GitHub Security Advisories path included, technical preview caveat included.
steps: Step 1: Select 2-5 individuals matching one or more GL-184 reviewer profiles (external-backend-developer, ai-agent-workflow-developer, grant-compliance-audit-reviewer, security-minded-technical-reviewer).
Step 2: Prepare the invite message using the GL-184 invite message draft. Verify all caveats and safety rules are present before sending.
Step 3: Send the invite message manually via a trusted private channel. Do not send via bulk email or automated tooling. Do not include in a public GitHub issue.
Step 4: Record the invite internally — reviewer profile archetype, invite sent date, and public commit hash at time of invite.
Step 5: Await response. Reviewers may submit via public GitHub issue or direct message. Direct security-sensitive feedback to GitHub Security Advisories.
Step 6: Capture each response using the feedback capture record model within 48 hours of receipt.
Step 7: Triage each response using the GL-183 category and severity model.

feedback_capture_record_model.feedback_id: string — unique ID for this feedback record (e.g. GL185-F001)
feedback_capture_record_model.invite_sent_date: date — ISO 8601 date the invite was sent (e.g. 2026-06-10)
feedback_capture_record_model.response_received_date: date — ISO 8601 date the response was received
feedback_capture_record_model.public_commit_reviewed: string — git commit hash at time of reviewer's review
feedback_capture_record_model.tasks_attempted: array of string — tasks the reviewer attempted from the GL-185 task list
feedback_capture_record_model.first_verifiable_output_attempted: boolean — whether the reviewer attempted the first verifiable output
feedback_capture_record_model.docs_clarity_score: integer 1-5 — self-reported clarity score (5 = very clear)
feedback_capture_record_model.trust_score: integer 1-5 — self-reported trust score (5 = high trust)
feedback_capture_record_model.broken_links_reported: array of string — links that returned 404 or were stale
feedback_capture_record_model.confusing_claims: array of string — claims that felt misleading or overclaiming
feedback_capture_record_model.requested_examples: array of string — examples the reviewer wanted
feedback_capture_record_model.category: string — GL-183 triage category (blocker, confusing-docs, broken-quickstart, missing-example, product-question, security-concern, production-readiness-concern, feature-request, non-scope-later)
feedback_capture_record_model.severity: string — critical / high / medium / low / info
feedback_capture_record_model.blocking: boolean — whether this item blocks future reviewer invites
feedback_capture_record_model.security_sensitive: boolean — whether the feedback contains security-sensitive content
feedback_capture_record_model.private_follow_up_required: boolean — whether a private follow-up channel is needed
feedback_capture_record_model.next_action: string — fix-now / clarify-docs / add-to-backlog / defer / close-as-non-scope / escalate-security
feedback_capture_record_model.notes: string — any additional context not captured in other fields

feedback_triage_flow.reference: GL-183 External Feedback Triage / Public Issue Hygiene

categories.use_when: Something prevents the reviewer from completing any task
Documentation is unclear, contradictory, or hard to follow
Clone/run path or first verifiable output does not work

categories.default_next_action: add-to-backlog or fix-now if it blocks comprehension

categories.use_when: The reviewer is asking what GrantLayer does or how it works
categories.default_next_action: answer publicly if safe and concise

categories.use_when: Potential security issue or sensitive reporting concern
Concern about production suitability, support, or operational maturity
Valid feedback out of scope for the current developer-preview posture

severity_levels.criteria: Serious issue affecting safety or first-use trust

routing: Critical/High + security-sensitive: Route immediately to GitHub Security Advisories. Do not discuss in public. Halt public engagement if a real secret or exploit detail was shared.
Broken quickstart (broken-quickstart, blocker): Treat as immediate fix candidate. Do not invite additional reviewers until the quickstart path is restored.
Confusing documentation (confusing-docs): Schedule a docs polish issue.
Feature request (feature-request): Backlog without commitment. Do not imply the feature will be built for developer-preview posture.
Production-readiness concern (production-readiness-concern): Clarify developer-preview posture. Do not promise production readiness, SLA, or onboarding.
Out of scope (non-scope-later): Close or defer politely. Explain the current posture.
Security advisory unavailable: If a reviewer cannot access GitHub Security Advisories, acknowledge publicly with a minimal non-revealing comment and open a private follow-up channel.

before_sending_any_invite: No secrets are requested from reviewers in the invite message.
No customer data is requested from reviewers.
No private grant or institutional data is requested.
The invite message does not claim production SaaS readiness.
The invite message does not claim tenant/workspace isolation is implemented.
The invite message does not offer a support SLA.
GitHub Security Advisories path is clearly stated in the invite message.
Reviewers are told to use synthetic/demo data only.
during_feedback_window: Do not ask reviewers to share real customer data in public issues.
Do not ask reviewers to share private grants or institutional records.
Do not post exploit details in public GitHub issues.
If a public issue contains secrets or exploit details, acknowledge and redirect immediately to GitHub Security Advisories. Do not quote or expand the sensitive content.
If a report includes a real secret, treat it as an incident or rotation concern before responding publicly.
Do not make production SaaS promises in public responses to feedback.
Do not make tenant isolation claims in public responses.
Do not make SLA or support commitments in public responses.
Record all feedback internally within 48 hours of receipt.
Triage all feedback using GL-183 category and severity model.
Stop the feedback window immediately if a critical-severity safety issue is found.

initial_reviewer_packet.project_description: GrantLayer is a Python backend with a REST API and SDK for creating structured, verifiable evidence bundles used in grant management and agentic compliance workflows.
initial_reviewer_packet.technical_preview_caveat: This is a technical preview / developer preview only. It is not a production SaaS platform. Tenant/workspace isolation is not yet implemented. No support SLA is offered.
initial_reviewer_packet.first_verifiable_output_task: git clone https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer.git && cd grantlayer && python3 examples/first_verifiable_output.py --output /tmp/gl_first_output.json — then compare /tmp/gl_first_output.json with examples/first_verifiable_output.json
feedback_questions: What did you think GrantLayer does after reading the README?
Could you find the first verifiable output quickly?
Could you run it, or understand what it proves?
Are the security reporting instructions clear?
What would make you try the SDK or API next?
What would make you trust this project more?
What is the smallest next example you would want?
safety_instructions: Do not share secrets, credentials, or private keys in public issues.
Do not share customer data or institutional records.
Do not post exploit details publicly.
Report security-sensitive issues via GitHub Security Advisories (see SECURITY.md).
initial_reviewer_packet.security_advisory_path: See SECURITY.md in the repository for the GitHub Security Advisories reporting path.

success_criteria: 2-5 reviewers invited manually using the GL-184 invite message draft
At least 1-3 reviewer responses captured in the feedback recording model within the feedback window
No secrets or customer data collected — all feedback uses synthetic/demo data only
No security-sensitive details posted publicly — any security concern routed to GitHub Security Advisories
Feedback categorized and severity-ranked using the GL-183 triage model for each captured response
Next issues proposed from the feedback — at minimum one follow-up issue identified from triage results
non_goals: No outreach sent by this issue. The invite message is defined in GL-184. This issue prepares the operational plan only.
No production SaaS promise. GrantLayer is in developer-preview / controlled-pilot posture only.
No tenant/workspace isolation claim. Tenant isolation is not implemented.
No real customer data collection. All feedback must use synthetic/demo data only.
No real grant/institutional data review. Reviewers must use synthetic identifiers.
No payment or treasury flow. Not in current MVP scope.
No blockchain requirement in current MVP. Planned as optional Phase 3 layer.
No SLA or support promise. No SLA is offered for developer preview.
No public issue template creation in this issue. Templates remain deferred.
No GitHub label creation in this issue. Labels are documented in GL-183; no API changes are made.
No GitHub issue creation in this issue. Issues are created by reviewers, not by this issue.
No automated collection of personal reviewer data. Do not collect names, email addresses, or contact details via any form, webhook, or API.

findings.recommendation: Open the feedback window by sending 2-5 invites manually using the GL-184 invite pack. Record all responses using the feedback capture record model. Do not widen the reviewer pool until at least 1 response is triaged.
